checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?
checks.guidance: Cross-check the result against explicit user notes, critique corrections, and watch items. Flag anything the implementation ignored, contradicted, or only partially covered.

findings.detail: Checked the shot-group selection behavior in [TimelineCanvas.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx#L1023), [TimelineEditor.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx#L874), and [TimelineCanvas.test.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx#L1015). The revised plan is correct to remove the extra canvas callback work, because single-click label selection and the existing `selectClips` wiring are already present in the repository.

Checked step 3 and the accompanying assumptions against [useSelectedMediaClips.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts) and [types/index.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts#L187). The revision still says timeline-selected clips can resolve shot membership from `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups`, but `ResolvedTimelineConfig` does not expose that field, so one of the original review findings remains only partially addressed.

prior_findings.detail: Filled in critique_output.json with findings after checking the selected-clip persistence path and the broader same-shot call sites.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?
checks.guidance: Look for logic errors, invalid assumptions, broken invariants, schema mismatches, or behavior that would fail at runtime. When the fix adds a conditional branch, check whether it handles all relevant cases — not just the one reported in the issue.

findings.detail: Checked [db.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/db.ts#L305), [types.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/types.ts#L56), and [tools/registry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/registry.ts#L49). The revised backend plan for loading `shotNamesById` into `TimelineState` and threading it through the `view_timeline` tool path is technically consistent with how timeline state and tool context are built today, so FLAG-001 looks genuinely addressed.

Checked step 3 against the actual `useSelectedMediaClips()` inputs in [useSelectedMediaClips.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts#L29). That hook currently reads only `selectedClipIds` and `resolvedConfig` from editor context, so the plan's literal instruction to join `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups` with `useShots()` data will not compile unless it first switches to `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` or another real source of pinned groups.

prior_findings.detail: Recorded that the plan assumes backend timeline config already has shot names and that `resolvedConfig` exposes `pinnedShotGroups`, but the repository does not support either assumption today.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?
checks.guidance: Look at how the changed function is used across the codebase. Does the fix only address one caller's scenario while others remain broken? Flag missing required work or out-of-scope edits. A minimal patch is often right, but check whether the underlying problem is bigger than what the issue describes.

findings.detail: Checked the same-shot call sites in [loop.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts#L381), [create-task.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts#L238), [loras.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/loras.ts#L111), and [duplicate-generation.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/duplicate-generation.ts#L14). The revision now scopes the shared shot-resolution helper across the broader agent surface that originally remained unaddressed, so FLAG-003 is materially fixed at the planning level.

Checked the frontend shot-resolution scope in [useSelectedMediaClips.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts), [TimelineEditor.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx#L191), and [usePinnedShotGroups.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts#L26). The revision narrowed away from duplicate canvas work correctly, but it still scopes the timeline clip lookup to a non-existent `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups` surface instead of the actual `data.config.pinnedShotGroups` path used elsewhere in the editor.

prior_findings.detail: Recorded that same-shot inference is broader than `duplicate_generation` and also affects loop context, create-task, and LoRA flows.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?
checks.guidance: Search for all instances of the symbol/pattern being changed. Also ask: does this feature require setup, registration, or integration code beyond the core logic? Missing glue code causes test failures even when the core fix is correct.

findings.detail: Checked the attachment-plumbing locations in [AgentChat.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx#L292), [useAgentSession.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts#L275), [selectedClips.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/selectedClips.ts#L32), [loop.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts#L72), and [llm/messages.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/llm/messages.ts#L23). The revised plan now explicitly covers the send path, normalization, and continuation recovery that were previously missing, so FLAG-002 looks addressed.

Checked the remaining frontend glue for resolving shot membership in [useSelectedMediaClips.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts#L29). Even with the broader attachment plumbing fixed, the location list is still incomplete because the plan points that hook at `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups`, while the actual pinned-group data lives on `data.config.pinnedShotGroups` and related helper paths such as [usePinnedShotGroups.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts#L26).

prior_findings.detail: Recorded missing glue locations including AgentChat send mapping, stored attachment schemas, selected-clip normalization, and supporting tests.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?
checks.guidance: Grep for call sites. For each caller, check what values it passes — especially edge cases like None, zero, empty, or composite inputs. Then ask: should this change be here, or in a caller, callee, or new method?

findings.detail: Checked the actual caller chain for outgoing attachments in [AgentChat.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx#L292) and [useAgentSession.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts#L275). The revised plan now names both caller layers, which is the right place to preserve `shotId`/`shotName` before they are converted to backend `shot_id`/`shot_name` fields.

Checked the caller contract of `useSelectedMediaClips()` via [useTimelineEditorData](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts#L29). This hook's caller currently provides `selectedClipIds` and `resolvedConfig`, not a resolved config object with `pinnedShotGroups`, so the plan still does not handle the actual arguments passed at that call site and needs to pull pinned groups from editor `data` instead.

prior_findings.detail: Recorded caller-specific risks for mixed gallery/timeline selections and continuation flows that recover attachments from stored turns.

flags.concern: Frontend shot lookup source: the revised plan still says `useSelectedMediaClips()` can read `pinnedShotGroups` from `resolvedConfig`, but that property does not exist on `ResolvedTimelineConfig`, so the step still points at the wrong state source.
